hooks.command: git -C "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-.}" push origin 2>&1 || echo '{"systemMessage": "git push failed — check remote"}'

jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""' | { read -r f; echo "$f" | grep -qE '(nbi_project_dashboard\.html|dashboard-server/)' && { curl -sf http://localhost:8888/nbi_project_dashboard.html -o /dev/null && echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"DASHBOARD EDIT DETECTED. You MUST now verify the dashboard loads correctly: (1) Run: curl -s http://localhost:8888/nbi_project_dashboard.html | head -5 to confirm it serves. (2) Check the browser console for JS errors — use Playwright MCP browser_navigate to http://localhost:8888/nbi_project_dashboard.html and browser_console_messages to check for errors. (3) Do NOT claim this change works until you have confirmed zero JS errors in the browser. If you skip this, you are violating Glen directive from 2026-05-25."}}' || echo '{"decision":"block","reason":"Dashboard server is not responding on port 8888. Cannot verify edit. Check PM2 status."}'; } || true; }

hooks.prompt: A subagent just completed work. The tool input and result are below. You MUST check: (1) Did the agent touch nbi_project_dashboard.html or any file in dashboard-server/? If yes, flag that the main context MUST verify the dashboard loads and check for JS errors before committing. (2) Did the agent make changes beyond the scope it was asked to handle? Flag any scope creep. (3) Did the agent leave uncommitted changes that could conflict with other work? (4) Are there any obvious bugs, typos, or broken references in the agent's output? Return ok=true only if the agent stayed in scope and produced reasonable output. Return ok=false with a clear reason if ANY of these checks fail. Input and result: $ARGUMENTS

hooks.command: CMD=$(python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('tool_input',d).get('command',''))" 2>/dev/null || true); case "$CMD" in *grep*|*rg\ *|*ripgrep*|*find\ *|*fd\ *|*ack\ *|*ag\ *)   [ -f graphify-out/graph.json ] &&   echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"graphify: knowledge graph at graphify-out/. For focused questions, run `graphify query \"<question>\"` (scoped subgraph, usually much smaller than GRAPH_REPORT.md) instead of grepping raw files. Read GRAPH_REPORT.md only for broad architecture context."}}'   || true ;; esac

HIT=$(python3 -c "import json,sys;d=json.load(sys.stdin);t=d.get('tool_input',d);s=(str(t.get('file_path') or '')+' '+str(t.get('pattern') or '')+' '+str(t.get('path') or '')).lower().replace(chr(92),'/');exts=('.py','.js','.ts','.tsx','.jsx','.go','.rs','.java','.rb','.c','.h','.cpp','.hpp','.cc','.cs','.kt','.swift','.php','.scala','.lua','.sh','.md','.rst','.txt','.mdx');sys.stdout.write('1' if 'graphify-out/' not in s and any(e in s for e in exts) else '')" 2>/dev/null || true); if [ "$HIT" = 1 ] && [ -f graphify-out/graph.json ]; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"graphify: knowledge graph at graphify-out/. For codebase questions, run `graphify query \"<question>\"` (scoped subgraph, usually much smaller than reading files one by one), `graphify explain \"<concept>\"`, or `graphify path \"<A>\" \"<B>\"`, instead of reading source files to answer. Read raw files to modify or debug specific code, or when the graph lacks the detail."}}'; fi || true
